arts.ttl: Derby County handed clear John Eustace next manager verdict after Blackburn statement
arts.sum: Derby County are in advanced talks to poach Blackburn Rovers manager John Eustace, after sacking Paul Warne last week amid a miserable run of results - and while fans in the Derbyshire Live comments section will be glad to see a new man to try to lead them clear of relegation trouble, they have been having their say on swapping potential promotion for a relegation battle. Eustace is reportedly disappointed by Blackburn's January transfer business, which the club addressed in an official statement, mentioning "significant investment being made during the January transfer window."

arts.ttl: Blackburn supporter group issues 'damning' statement with John Eustace set for Derby County
arts.sum: To lose him from a play-off position to a team currently in the relegation zone is a damning indictment of everything that is wrong with our club. Derby are currently in the relegation zone and Saturday’s 1-1 draw at Norwich City arrested a seven-game losing run.

arts.ttl: Costa Rican says QPR rejection opened door to join Derby County – Initially heading elsewhere
arts.sum: Paulo Wanchope has told Spanish newspaper AS that he approached Queens Park Rangers before joining Derby County. The Costa Rican first arrived in Europe when Derby County signed him in 1997 when they were in the Premier League. At that time, QPR were in the second division. Wanchope also had spells at West Ham United and The post Costa Rican says QPR rejection opened door to join Derby County – Initially heading elsewhere appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: ‘May come to bite them’ – Derby County could struggle against Oxford United, EFL pundit says
arts.sum: Derby County could struggle against their former manager Gary Rowett’s Oxford United side, EFL pundit Sam Parkin believes.… The post ‘May come to bite them’ – Derby County could struggle against Oxford United, EFL pundit says appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Mark Harris on life under Gary Rowett and new competition up front
arts.sum: MARK Harris says that Oxford United becoming a tougher team to beat has been one of the key reasons behind Gary Rowett’s early success in improving the side.

arts.ttl: Darren Bent delivers Paul Warne sack verdict and makes John Eustace prediction
arts.sum: Darren Bent has backed John Eustace to keep Derby County in the Championship if he is appointed Paul Warne’s successor at Pride Park. That impression has only grown following successful spells in charge of Birmingham City and Blackburn Rovers, with the Ewood Park outfit confirming Eustace has asked to be given permission to speak to Derby.

arts.ttl: John Eustace: Blackburn Rovers hit out at boss amid desire to speak to Derby County
arts.sum: Blackburn Rovers release an official statement to express their disappointment at John Eustace being open to becoming the new head coach of Derby County.

arts.ttl: 'Extremely disappointed' Blackburn release John Eustace statement amid Derby County links
arts.sum: Join our Derby County WhatsApp group to get the latest Rams headlines sent straight to your phone. Eustace, thought to be disappointed by a less than inspiring transfer window at Ewood Park, then held talks with Rovers' hierarchy this afternoon about his desire to speak to Derby and the Championsip club say they have "reluctantly granted" that request.

arts.ttl: Blackburn boss Eustace set for Derby move
arts.sum: Blackburn Rovers "reluctantly" give boss John Eustace permission to speak to Derby County about their managerial vacancy.

arts.ttl: Matt Hamshaw primed for duty as Derby County pursue John Eustace
arts.sum: Interim boss Matt Hamshaw says he is comfortable with being in charge of Derby County's clash against Oxford tomrrow night and insists the club are working hard as they can to appoint a new manager. Asked this afternoon about the situation and whether there was a timeframe he was working to, Hamshaw said: "No and I am quite comfortable with that and they are too.
